ogTitle: Stroud Road vs Stuarts Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Stroud Road and Stuarts Point.
heading: Stroud Road vs Stuarts Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stroud Road, New South Wales and Stuarts Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stuarts Point has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Stroud Road (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stroud Road or Stuarts Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stroud Road has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Stuarts Point scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stroud Road scores 32/100 for bushfire risk. Stuarts Point scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stroud Road or Stuarts Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stuarts Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
